articles.file: The Art of Pushing Emotional Buttons.pdf
articles.title: The Art of Pushing Emotional Buttons
articles.description: On the quiet, deliberate ways people learn exactly which words will land, and what it costs to know someone that well.

articles.title: Understanding Is Not Releasing
articles.description: A reflection on how self awareness can quietly become a form of control, where emotions are fully understood but rarely fully felt.

articles.file: When You Outgrow The Version Your Parents Remember.pdf
articles.title: When You Outgrow The Version Your Parents Remember
articles.description: Not a crisis, not distance, just the quiet realization that as you grow, you become someone your parents are still getting to know.

articles.file: The Day "Can I" Becomes, I'm Doing This.pdf
articles.title: The Day "Can I" Becomes, I'm Doing This
articles.description: Independence should not be this scary. But it is. Can we talk about how hard it is to start reporting instead of asking for permission.

articles.file: The Quiet Rebellion of Growing Up.pdf
articles.title: The Quiet Rebellion of Growing Up
articles.description: On another episode of growing up, we look at how that has impacted our relationships with our parents.

articles.title: The Delusions We Carried Into 25
articles.description: The internal version of growing up — when the beliefs you held about love and work start dissolving quietly, like sugar in hot tea.

articles.title: Not Lost. Just 25
articles.description: I didn't care about politics before. Then I turned 25 and the world started feeling like something I was supposed to have an opinion on.

articles.title: Two Timelines in One Bed
articles.description: A blunt look at the finish line myth and the comedic silence that follows when two people are technically together but secretly decades apart.

articles.file: Men, Silence, and the Moment After.pdf
articles.title: Men, Silence, and the Moment After
articles.description: The system reboot from 'Epic Romance' to 'Local Hardware Store Catalog'. On men, emotional vocabulary, and what lives in the silence.

articles.file: Why Humans are Bad at Debugging Their Own Lives.pdf
articles.title: Why Humans are Bad at Debugging Their Own Lives
articles.description: An analysis of our emotional glitches and faulty code, and why we are always the last to find the bug in our own system.

articles.title: Emotional Multitasking
articles.description: The tension of being caught between two worlds, the one you're in and the one you're still carrying from before.

articles.title: Paying the Passion Tax
articles.description: Why we walk back into collapsing buildings. On the cost of caring, and whether some rooms are worth every penny.

articles.title: Fireworks
articles.description: A short reflection on ignition and temporary light, on what it means to be briefly, spectacularly seen, and then to go dark.

articles.title: The Cost of Safe Words
articles.description: The safety found in silence and withheld words. What we protect when we don't say things, and what we lose in the keeping.

articles.title: Things I've Noticed Lately
articles.description: Small details ignored in the noise of being human. A collection of observations from the edge of attention.

articles.file: The space between words and meaning.pdf
articles.title: The Space Between Words
articles.description: The gap where communication fails and something truer takes its place. On meaning, silence, and what gets lost in translation.

articles.title: Your Voice
articles.description: Finding your unique sound in a crowded world. On why the thing that makes you strange is the only thing worth amplifying.

articles.file: Can I Tell You How That Made Me Feel.pdf
articles.title: Can I Tell You How That Made Me Feel
articles.description: On the difference between reacting and actually naming what something did to you, and why that honesty is harder than it sounds.

articles.file: Can You Love Someone So Much That It Hurts.pdf
articles.title: Can You Love Someone So Much That It Hurts?
articles.description: For anyone who's ever loved deeply, questioned everything, and wondered why the heart can feel both full and heavy at the same time.

articles.title: The Art of Being Chosen
articles.description: What does it really mean to be chosen? A heartfelt journey through the small moments, quiet gestures, and universal longing that connect us all.

poems.title: Devotion's Cost
poems.description: Can it ever be too much giving for one person?

poems.title: Unfamiliar Air
poems.description: The worst part about being loved right is the fear part. Wrote this for that feeling you have when someone right feels wrong.

poems.title: How I Love You
poems.description: The vulnerability of affection and the weight of the heart. Written for no one. Written for someone.

poems.title: The Scary Part of Being Loved
poems.description: Transitioning from survival to safety, and the terror of finally believing someone when they stay.

poems.title: When Someone Feels Like Home
poems.description: Choosing every page of a story that stretches long. On the quiet miracle of recognising someone.

poems.title: Eloping
poems.description: A love that dared to leave everything behind. On choosing each other over the noise of everyone else's opinion.

poems.file: the one I let get away.pdf
poems.title: Let Get Away
poems.description: To the one I was too unsure to keep. A poem about hesitation, and the particular weight of the almost.

poems.title: Salt and Surrender
poems.description: When dawn arrives, we'll keep the quiet. A poem about the way tenderness makes us undone.

books.title: Almost, Always
books.description: A book about choices and the paths we almost took. The ones we chose and the ones that chose us. Everything we nearly were.

books.title: Unspoken Word
books.description: A story about secrets and hidden truths, the words we swallow and the ones that escape without permission.
